findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-COB-001
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-COB-001 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-COB-005
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-COB-005 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: PS-COB-003 (Credit Assessment) has no control linked
findings.detail: Every other substantive step has at least one named control: PS-COB-001 has CP-COB-003, PS-COB-002 has CP-COB-001 and CP-COB-002, PS-COB-004 has CP-COB-004, PS-COB-005 has CP-COB-005. PS-COB-003 is the only non-quality-check process step with no control, leaving the credit decisioning activity uncontrolled from a formal compliance perspective.

findings.title: PS-COB-001 body contains junk text
findings.detail: The 'What happens' section of PS-COB-001 ends with the spurious characters 'fghfh' appended to the final sentence: '...Incomplete applications are flagged and returned to the client before they consume pipeline capacity.fghfh'. This is a data-entry artifact that should be removed.

findings.title: FP-COB-004 missing addressedBy back-link to II-COB-007
findings.detail: II-COB-007 (Conversational KYC assistant for ownership questions) declares addresses: [FP-COB-004], but FP-COB-004 (Complex Ownership Questions) has no addressedBy field. The other three friction points all carry a reciprocal addressedBy link; FP-COB-004 is the only one where the back-link is absent.

findings.title: II-COB-009 and II-COB-011 have no friction-point or pain-point in their addresses list
findings.detail: II-COB-009 (Real-time SLA and per-step timing dashboard) addresses only process gaps PG-COB-001 and PG-COB-003; II-COB-011 (Continuous controls monitoring) addresses only control gaps CG-COB-001 and CG-COB-002. Every other innovation idea traces to at least one FP or PP. Is it intentional that these two ideas have no FP/PP link, or should each be associated with a pain-point such as the operational and staff pain behind the missing measurement and the audit-evidence gap?

findings.title: SYS-COB-006 (CDS) and SYS-COB-007 (DMS) have no documented integrations
findings.detail: Both systems list integrates: []. CDS is the credit decisioning system used in PS-COB-003; it must receive KYC-cleared data and return a credit decision, yet no integration element captures how. DMS (OpenText) stores application documents from intake through KYC and is referenced in PS-COB-001's systems list, yet has no documented connection to CRM, OWS, or the KYC platform. Are these integrations deliberately omitted (e.g. file-share or manual hand-off), or are INT elements still to be written?

findings.title: SYS-COB-007 (DMS) not listed in PS-COB-002 systems
findings.detail: SYS-COB-007's own description says it 'holds the application documents from intake through KYC', implying it is accessed during both PS-COB-001 and PS-COB-002. However PS-COB-002's systems field lists only SYS-COB-003 and SYS-COB-004. Does the KYC Analyst access DMS during identity verification, or do documents move entirely into the KYC platform (SYS-COB-003) at intake so that DMS plays no active role in PS-COB-002?
